content.full_text:
Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH


August 27, 1990


Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:  Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     This firm represents several candidates and
committees who are required to file campaign statements
pursuant to the Political Reform Act.  On behalf of our
clients, we would like to request your reconsideration
of prior advice provided by the FPPC regarding the
proper reporting for committee expenditures to slate
mailer organizations.

     The only advice letter on this subject appears to
be a 1988 advice letter.  (A-88-283)  At the
requestor's suggestion, the advice was given that
candidates and committees who make expenditures to
slate mailer organizations should include in their
campaign statements a copy of the Disbursements
Schedule (D-1) from the slate mailer organization's
campaign disclosure statement (Form 401).

     Compliance with this advice has proven to be
burdensome and sometimes confusing.  For preelection
reports where there is a five-day turn around, it is
often difficult to obtain the copies of the slate
mailer's report in a timely fashion.  Also inclusion of
the slate mailer Disbursement Schedule which lists all
payments made in connection with a slate mailer is
often confusing since the committee may have spent only
a minimal amount to be on the slate.  For example, a
ballot measure committee paid $200 to be on a
particular slate mailer, and the total expenditures by
the slate mailer organization exceeded $1 million.
Inclusion of the slate mailer organizations's
Disbursements Schedule created a misleading impression
that the committee had made those expenditures.


LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH
GEORGE M. WATERS
DIANE M. FISHBURN

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member
California State Legislature


300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280

Scott Hallabrin
August 27, 1990
Page Two

     Also, there were instances where the committee had made a
payment to be on a slate mailer, but the slate mailer
organization had not yet made any expenditures during the period
in question. It is unclear whether the committee must amend their
advice under the current statute or include the slate mailer organization's Disbursement
statement to include the slate mailer organization's Disbursement
Schedule when expenditures are made or include those with a
future campaign statement.

     We believe that these results were not intended by the FPPC.
Moreover, this type of disclosure does not serve any of the Act's
purposes since all of the payments and receipts for a slate
mailer are reported by the slate mailer organization on its
campaign statement.

     Accordingly, we request that you consider modifying the
advice given in 1988. We suggest that, for these expenditures,
committees report the name, address and identification number of
the slate mailer organization (or, if no identification number is
the slate mailer organization (or, if no identification number is
available, the name and telephone number of the treasurer), the
amount of the expenditure, and a description (i.e., "Slate
Mailer").

     Thank you for your consideration of this request.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL,
FONG & LEIDIGH

[signature]
DIANE M. FISHBURN

DMF:sk

embedding.qa_text: August 27, 1990 Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Request for Advice Dear Mr. Hallabrin: This firm represents several candidates and committees who are required to file campaign statements pursuant to the Political Reform Act. On behalf of our clients, we would like to request your reconsideration of prior advice provided by the FPPC regarding the proper reporting for committee expenditures to slate mailer organizations. The only advice letter on this subject appears to be a 1988 advice letter. (A-88-283) At the requestor's suggestion, the advice was given that candidates and committees who make expenditures to slate mailer organizations should include in their campaign statements a copy of the Disbursements Schedule (B-1) from the slate mailer organization's campaign disclosure statement (Form 401). Compliance with this advice has proven to be burdensome and sometimes confusing. For pre-election reports where there is a five-day turn around, it is often difficult to obtain the copies of the slate mailer's report in a timely fashion. Also inclusion of the slate mailer Disbursement Schedule which lists all payments made in connection with a slate mailer is often confusing since the committee may have spent only a minimal amount to be on the slate. For example, a ballot measure committee paid $200 to be on a particular slate mailer, and the total expenditures by the slate mailer organization exceeded $1 million. Inclusion of the slate mailer organization's Disbursements Schedule created a misleading impression that the committee had made those expenditures. Scott Hallabrin August 27, 1990 Page Two Also, there were instances where the committee had made a payment to be on a slate mailer, but the slate mailer organization had not yet made any expenditures during the period covered by the committee's campaign statement. It is unclear under the current advice whether the committee must amend their statement to include the slate mailer organization's Disbursement Schedule when expenditures are made or include those with a future campaign statement. We believe that these results were not intended by the FPPC. Moreover, this type of disclosure does not serve any of the Act's purposes since all of the payments and receipts for a slate mailer are reported by the slate mailer organization on its campaign statement. Accordingly, we request that you consider modifying the advice given in 1988. We suggest that, for these expenditures, committees report the name, address and identification number of the slate mailer organization (or, if no identification number is available, the name and telephone number of the treasurer), the amount of the expenditure, and a description (i.e., "Slate Mailer"). Thank you for your consideration of this request. Very truly yours, OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH [Signature] DIANE M. FISHBURN DMF:sk
